reasoning: The threshold requires >124.17 protests in a 30-day window, given the 12.417 reference value (10 times the 1-plus-average of 11.417 monthly protests over the prior year). That is >4 protests per day, or more than 90% of a typical year's total protests crammed into a single month. Recent 2025 protests regarding an anti-LGBTQ law and the political rise of Peter Magyar have led to large weekly rallies in Budapest, but not the widespread daily, multi-city events needed to hit 125 unique ACLED events in 30 days. Reaching this threshold would require either an unprecedented national uprising or a massive decentralized campaign (like a daily cross-country opposition tour logged as separate protests). Since this is highly unusual for Hungary, chances are very low for the near-term spring dates, though the probability increases slightly towards late 2025 (October) as the 2026 election campaign heats up and uncertainty grows.
